---
title: "ANX_manhattan_plot"
output: html_document
author: Julia Lohman
---

#------------------- Generating the Manhattan Plot (fig. 1) --------------------
```{r}
# Manhattan plot of anxiety GWAS data each dot is a SNP plotted by genome position 
library('readxl')
ANX_data <- read_excel('ANX_2026_daner_fullANX_v12_woUTAH_11022026.gz.p4.clump.areator.sorted.1mhc (1).xls')
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
ANX_data %>%
  select(SNP, CHR, BP, P) %>% 
  mutate(CHR = as.factor(CHR)) %>% 
  arrange(CHR,BP) %>% 
  #chromosome numbers have been multiplied by 100 million to separate them visually on the graph
  mutate(Chromosome_Position = as.numeric(CHR)*1000000000+BP) %>% 
  
  #y intercept denotes a line of significance; from the article we pulled the data from
  #line marks the significance threshold (p < 5e-8); dots above it are significant hits.
  ggplot(aes(Chromosome_Position, -log10(P), color= CHR)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) + geom_hline(yintercept = 7.30102999566, color = 'darkblue', size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  
  labs(title = "Manhattan Plot") +
  
theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(family = "serif"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
 
```
